news_articles.content:
Analysts raise Marvell to Buy as outlook signals growth re-acceleration
Vahid Karaahmetovic
Fri, March 6, 2026 at 7:34 AM CST
Investing.com -- Analysts at Benchmark and Bank of America upgraded Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) to Buy after the chipmaker���s latest results and outlook reinforced expectations for accelerating AI-driven growth.
Marvell Technology on Thursday projected fiscal 2028 (FY28) revenue of nearly $15 billion, signaling strong demand for the company���s custom chips and interconnect products used in AI data centers. The outlook exceeded Wall Street estimates of $12.92 billion and pushed the stock up more than 11% in Friday premarket trading.
The company also lifted its fiscal 2027 outlook, now expecting revenue growth of more than 30% year over year to nearly $11 billion, up from its previous forecast of around $10 billion.
Following the release, Bank of America (BofA) lifted its rating to Buy from Neutral and the price target to $110 from $90, citing improving visibility across several key AI-related drivers.
Marvell���s latest earnings call increased confidence in the company���s ���solid leverage to AI optical connectivity,��� as well as the potential success of an upcoming Microsoft custom chip program and signs that the company is ���turning the corner on Amazon XPU transition year,��� analysts led by Vivek Arya said.
BofA lifted its longer-term forecasts, raising FY27 and FY28 sales estimates by roughly 8% and 12%, respectively, while increasing EPS

news_articles.title: BlackRock Writes Down $25M Loan to Zero, Raising Private Credit Concerns
news_articles.content:
BlackRock Writes Down $25M Loan to Zero, Raising Private Credit Concerns
Khac Phu Nguyen
Fri, March 6, 2026 at 6:09 AM CST
This article first appeared on
.
BlackRock (
) is
fresh scrutiny in private credit after a recent filing showed its lending arm wrote down the value of a $25 million loan to zero just three months after valuing it at par. The adjustment came through BlackRock TCP Capital, a business development company sponsored by the asset manager, which held a second-lien loan to Infinite Commerce Holdings, an Amazon aggregator focused on acquiring online sellers. In a third-quarter filing, the loan had been marked at 100 cents on the dollar before the subsequent change disclosed in the company's latest 10-K.
The writedown follows another full markdown within the same platform, after BlackRock TCP previously wrote down loans made to Renovo Home Partners. While the Infinite Commerce exposure is small relative to BlackRock's overall scale, the development could highlight a broader dynamic in private credit markets, where valuations for illiquid loans may lag behind the weakening performance of the businesses those loans support. The situation has added to ongoing debate about risk within direct lending strategies.
Concerns about the resilience of private credit have been building in recent months. JPMorgan Chase (
) CEO Jamie Dimon has warned that bankruptcies involving companies such as Tricolor and First Brands may signal additional stress within the sector, though som

news_articles.title: BlackRock Expands iBonds ETFs And Launches Leveraged Loan Fund As Shares Lag
news_articles.content:
BlackRock Expands iBonds ETFs And Launches Leveraged Loan Fund As Shares Lag
Simply Wall St
Fri, March 6, 2026 at 3:13 AM CST
in any market cycle. Join 7 million investors using Simply Wall St's investing ideas for FREE.
BlackRock has announced a broad expansion of its iBonds ETF platform across Treasuries, TIPS, corporates, high yield, and municipal bonds.
The company is also launching the iShares Broad USD Floating Rate Loan ETF, its first index based ETF providing access to U.S. leveraged loans.
These launches highlight new ways for investors to approach bond laddering and gain index based access to the leveraged loan market.
BlackRock, trading on the NYSE under ticker NYSE:BLK, is rolling out these products while its shares trade at about $1,035.0. The stock has gained 10.7% over the past year and 61.5% over five years, while showing a 70.6% return over three years. In the short term, the share price has seen a 5.1% decline over the past week and a 4.6% decline year to date.
For investors watching fixed income and credit markets, these product launches may be worth tracking as they could influence how money is allocated across bonds and leveraged loans. The broader iBonds range and the new floating rate loan ETF could change how both individual and institutional investors think about building bond ladders, managing interest rate exposure, and accessing parts of the loan market that can be harder to reach with traditional tools.

Semiconductor solutions revenues (64.8% of net revenues) totaled $12.52 billion, up 52% year over year. The uptick was driven by the strong AI revenues, which surged 106% year over year. Enterprise networking, broadband and server storage revenues were up year over year, offset by a seasonal decline in wireless.
Infrastructure software revenues (35.2% of net revenues) climbed 1% year over year to $6.8 billion. VMware revenues grew 13% year over year. Bookings continue to be strong, and total contract value booked in the fiscal first quarter exceeded $9.2 billion.

news_articles.title: Teradyne (TER) Up 7.5% Since Last Earnings Report: Can It Continue?
news_articles.content:
Teradyne (TER) Up 7.5% Since Last Earnings Report: Can It Continue?
Zacks Equity Research
Wed, March 4, 2026 at 10:30 AM CST
It has been about a month since the last earnings report for Teradyne (TER). Shares have added about 7.5% in that time frame, outperforming the S&P 500.
Will the recent positive trend continue leading up to its next earnings release, or is Teradyne due for a pullback? Before we dive into how investors and analysts have reacted as of late, let's take a quick look at the latest earnings report in order to get a better handle on the important drivers.
Teradyne Q4 Earnings Top Estimates, Revenues Up Y/Y
Teradyne reported fourth-quarter 2025 non-GAAP earnings of $1.80 per share, which beat the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 32.22% and surged 89.5% year over year.
Revenues of $1.08 billion beat the Zacks Consensus Estimate by 11.82% and increased 43.7% year over year.
TER’s Q4 Top-line Details
Revenues from Semiconductor Test platforms, Robotics, and Product Test were $883 million (81.6% of total revenues), $89 million (8.2%), and $110 million (10.2%), respectively.
In the fourth quarter, Teradyne reported a non-GAAP gross margin of 57.3%, which contracted 210 basis points (bps) year over year.
In the reported quarter, selling and administrative expenses increased 5.7% year over year to $164.7 million. As a percentage of revenues, selling and marketing expenses were 15.2%, down 550 bps year over year.
Engineering and development expenses increased 11.6% year ove

news_articles.title: ON Semiconductor Corporation (ON) is Attracting Investor Attention: Here is What You Should Know
news_articles.content:
ON Semiconductor Corporation (ON) is Attracting Investor Attention: Here is What You Should Know
Zacks Equity Research
Wed, March 4, 2026 at 8:00 AM CST
ON Semiconductor Corp.
(ON) has recently been on Zacks.com's list of the most searched stocks. Therefore, you might want to consider some of the key factors that could influence the stock's performance in the near future.
Shares of this semiconductor components maker have returned +6.7% over the past month versus the Zacks S&P 500 composite's -1.3% change. The Zacks Semiconductor - Analog and Mixed industry, to which ON Semiconductor Corp. belongs, has gained 2.8% over this period. Now the key question is: Where could the stock be headed in the near term?
Although media reports or rumors about a significant change in a company's business prospects usually cause its stock to trend and lead to an immediate price change, there are always certain fundamental factors that ultimately drive the buy-and-hold decision.
Earnings Estimate Revisions
Rather than focusing on anything else, we at Zacks prioritize evaluating the change in a company's earnings projection. This is because we believe the fair value for its stock is determined by the present value of its future stream of earnings.
Our analysis is essentially based on how sell-side analysts covering the stock are revising their earnings estimates to take the latest business trends into account. When earnings estimates for a company go up, the fair value for its stock goes up as w

on ON Semiconductor Corporation on X.com by @MoneyShow. In this article, we will summarize the bulls��� thesis on ON. ON Semiconductor Corporation's share was trading at $69.68 as of February 25th. ON���s trailing and forward P/E were 46.42 and 17.01, respectively according to Yahoo Finance.

Photo by JESHOOTS.COM on Unsplash
ON Semiconductor Corporation provides intelligent sensing and power solutions in Hong Kong, Singapore, the United Kingdom, the United States, and internationally. ON is positioned as a differentiated semiconductor leader through its focus on gallium nitride (GaN) and silicon carbide (SiC) technologies rather than traditional silicon-based chips.
These advanced materials enable higher voltage tolerance, greater thermal performance, and superior power density, making ON���s solutions critical for intelligent power management, sensing, and high-efficiency systems across electric vehicles, industrial automation, and energy infrastructure. SiC, in particular, is becoming foundational in advanced image sensors, robotics, medical imaging, smart grids, and next-generation power systems, while adoption in data centers and networking continues accelerating, with AI-driven demand adding further momentum.
Despite cyclical softness in 2025 among automakers

news_articles.title: ON Semiconductor Refocuses On Higher Margin Growth And Cash Generation
news_articles.content:
ON Semiconductor Refocuses On Higher Margin Growth And Cash Generation
Simply Wall St
Sat, February 28, 2026 at 10:09 AM CST
in any market cycle. Join 7 million investors using Simply Wall St's investing ideas for FREE.
ON Semiconductor (NasdaqGS:ON) is shifting capital and resources toward higher margin segments such as silicon carbide and AI data center power solutions.
The company is tightening capital discipline and prioritizing projects that target stronger returns on investment.
This repositioning comes as demand cools in core markets including electric vehicles and smartphones.
ON Semiconductor, a key supplier of power and sensing chips, is recalibrating its business mix as parts of the automotive and consumer electronics markets face softer demand. Management is leaning into areas like silicon carbide, which is used in power electronics, and solutions for AI data centers where power efficiency is a core requirement. At the same time, the company is putting more scrutiny on how and where it deploys capital.
For you as an investor, an important consideration is how this shift toward higher margin projects and tighter spending could influence the company���s earnings quality and free cash flow over time. The coming quarters will help clarify how quickly the new focus areas scale and how effectively ON Semiconductor manages ongoing sector headwinds.

in any market cycle. Join 7 million investors using Simply Wall St's investing ideas for FREE.

news_articles.title: Why Wall Street is calling out ‘echoes’ of the 2008 financial crisis
news_articles.content:
Craig T Fruchtman/Getty Images
Link Copied!
Follow
—
For months, investors and analysts have kept a close eye on the shadowy corner of finance known as private credit, where alarm bells have stoked fears of a repeat of the 2008 financial crisis.
Whether those alarms amount to a handful of isolated bad bets or a more menacing systemic weakness in the $1.8 trillion sector is far from clear. But if the latter is even a remote possibility, it���s worth understanding what the heck is going on.
A quick primer on ���private credit���
Very simply, the term refers to investors lending money directly to private businesses, bypassing banks. The borrowers ��� mostly smaller companies that banks would consider too risky or complex for a traditional loan ��� pay a higher interest rate in exchange for quick access to capital and flexible financing terms.
Here���s how it usually works: Big asset managers (think Blackstone, better known for buying companies outright) pool funds from big investors like pensions or insurance companies looking for higher returns than they can find in, say, the bond market. Those private-credit funds lend money directly to businesses that may otherwise struggle to get loans.
It���s not a new practice, but it became a much bigger business after the 2008 financial crisis, when governments tightened lending restrictions on banks.
Trouble is, private credit problems can quickly become public problems.
���You���ve got an opaque set of loans, in many cases, backing an

news_articles.title: Blackstone Redemptions Test Liquidity As Shares Trade Below Fair Value
news_articles.content:
Blackstone Redemptions Test Liquidity As Shares Trade Below Fair Value
Simply Wall St
Thu, March 5, 2026 at 12:13 AM CST
with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.
Blackstone's flagship private credit fund, BCRED, has seen a sharp rise in investor redemption requests.
In response, Blackstone raised the fund's repurchase cap and injected additional capital to meet all redemption demands.
The decision to fully honor requests and support the fund with fresh capital marks an unusual move in the private credit market.
For investors watching NYSE:BX, this comes at a time when the share price is around $114.14 and recent stock performance has been weak, with a 19.2% decline over the past 30 days and a 28.1% decline year to date. Over a 5-year period, however, the stock is up 91.3%. This highlights how long-term holders have experienced a very different journey from recent entrants.
BCRED's redemption spike and Blackstone's response put a spotlight on liquidity, transparency, and how private credit funds handle stressed conditions. As the situation develops, investors may focus on how consistently Blackstone applies these measures, how it communicates fund mechanics, and whether this episode changes perceptions of risk and access to capital in NYSE:BX linked vehicles.

news_articles.title: 3 Things Every Strategy Investor Needs to Know
Roundhill’s 50% Yield Innovation-100 ETF Is Waiting To Step on A Landmine
news_articles.content:
Roundhill Innovation-100 0DTE Covered Call Strategy ETF (
) was built for a specific type of investor: someone who wants meaningful income from tech exposure without simply riding the Nasdaq up and down. The fund sells zero-days-to-expiration covered calls on the Innovation-100 Index each morning, collects the premium, and distributes it weekly. With roughly $927 million in assets and a strategy that has attracted a devoted income-seeking following, QDTE has delivered. But the architecture of the fund creates a specific landmine that most holders are not thinking about clearly.
The covered call premium cushions losses slightly on bad days. It does not stop them. And the holdings underneath this fund are not your average blue chips sitting quietly in a portfolio. They are some of the most volatile, highest-multiple, binary-outcome names in the market.
The Upside Cap Does Not Come With a Downside Floor
The primary risk facing QDTE is straightforward but easy to underestimate: the fund’s underlying holdings can fall sharply, and the daily options premium collected does almost nothing to offset a serious drawdown. Selling a 0DTE covered call generates a small amount of daily income. It does not generate a hedge. If a core holding drops sharply in a week, a few days of premium income will not make a dent.
Strategy / MicroStrategy (MSTR)
Consider what is actually sitting in this fund. Strategy (
), formerly MicroStrategy, is essentially a leveraged bitcoin vehicle. In Q4 2025, the

How The Archrock (AROC) Story Is Shifting With Higher Targets And Evolving Market Perception
Simply Wall St
Thu, March 5, 2026 at 1:08 AM CST
with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.
Archrock���s updated fair value estimate has been lifted from US$32.22 to US$36.67, signaling a higher assessed price target in the latest analyst work. That shift lines up with recent Street commentary where several bullish firms are now talking about targets in the high US$30s to US$40 range, tied to their views on execution, profitability and how the market may classify the business. As you read on, you will see how these moving pieces fit together and how to keep track of the evolving Archrock story.
What Wall Street Has Been Saying
���� Bullish Takeaways
Citi, Stifel and Raymond James have all lifted their Archrock price targets into the US$40 range, signaling that several large research desks see room above the prior low US$30s targets.
Citi describes Archrock as "firing on all cylinders" after the Q4 beat, which points to analyst confidence around recent execution and profitability.
Raymond James highlights what it views as an industry leading balance sheet and continued capital discipline, and sees room for Archrock to generate free cash flow even while funding its stated dividend plans.

news_articles.title: Carvana (CVNA) Stock Continues to Slide As Analysts Lower Price Targets
news_articles.content:
Carvana (CVNA) Stock Continues to Slide As Analysts Lower Price Targets
Jabran Kundi
Wed, March 4, 2026 at 6:43 PM CST
Carvana Co. (NYSE:CVNA) is one of the
.
On February 20, Citi analyst Ronald Josey maintained a Buy rating on Carvana Co. (NYSE:CVNA) while lowering the firm���s price target from $550 to $465. According to the firm, the downward price target adjustment is due to the increased market volatility following the company���s earnings report. Citi also believes that the company���s outlook for the first quarter and for the full-year 2026 will be cautious. The analyst said that the firm would consider buying the stock if it experiences any weakness in the short term.
In addition to Citi, UBS also reduced its price target on Carvana Co. (NYSE:CVNA) from $545 to $485 on February 20 while keeping a Buy rating. The analyst said that the company missed its Q4 EBITDA expectations due to higher reconditioning costs, which are considered temporary. However, in the short term, retail GPU and EBITDA per unit are projected to stay flat or slightly decline compared with the last year.
Carvana (CVNA) Stock Continues to Slide As Analysts Lower Price Targets
Carvana Co. (NYSE:CVNA) operates an e-commerce platform for buying and selling used cars. The company is based in Tempe, Arizona, and was founded in 2012 by Ernest Garcia III, Benjamin Huston, and Ryan Keeton.

How The DoorDash (DASH) Investment Story Is Shifting With New Spending Plans And Analyst Revisions
Simply Wall St

with Simply Wall St's easy and powerful screener, trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.
DoorDash���s fair value estimate has been trimmed from $274.69 to $260.90, a cut of about 5% in the latest model update. This shift lines up with Street research, where many firms are lowering price targets after recent Q4 results, Q1 guidance, and management���s plan for heavier investment through 2026, even as some keep positive ratings and others lean more cautious. As you read on, you will see how these moving targets fit into the evolving analyst story and how to keep track of it as an investor.
What Wall Street Has Been Saying
���� Bullish Takeaways
Several firms, including Citi, Benchmark, BofA and KeyBanc, keep Buy or Overweight ratings while adjusting price targets, pointing to strong Gross Order Volume trends, newer verticals moving toward profitability and what they see as a long runway across grocery, retail and international.
Truist, Benchmark and KeyBanc highlight DoorDash���s broader ambition to be an operating system for local commerce and a global platform. They view Q4 results and Q1 guidance as consistent with that build out, even as near term EBITDA guidance reflects heavier spending.

news_articles.title: James Hunter to Transition from Role as General Counsel After Nearly 20 Years with Marriott Vacations Worldwide
news_articles.content:
This is a paid press release. Contact the press release distributor directly with any inquiries.
James Hunter to Transition from Role as General Counsel After Nearly 20 Years with Marriott Vacations Worldwide
Business Wire
Fri, March 6, 2026 at 7:58 AM CST
ORLANDO, Fla., March 06, 2026
--(
)--Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corporation (NYSE: VAC) ("MVW" or the "Company") announced today that, after nearly 20 years with the Company, Executive Vice President and General Counsel, James Hunter ("Jim") will transition from his role on March 9, 2026 and retire on April 1, 2026.
"On behalf of our Board of Directors and associates, I thank Jim for his long���standing service and the contributions he has made to our Company," said Matt Avril, Chief Executive Officer of MVW.
Hunter began his in���house legal career at Marriott International, Inc. ("Marriott") in 1994, supporting global development and expansion initiatives. After leading legal support for Marriott���s growth across Asia���Pacific, he relocated to Orlando in 2006 to lead the Law Department of Marriott Vacation Club International, overseeing significant expansion and the launch of the Marriott Vacation Club Destinations points product. He subsequently played a key role in the 2011 spin���off that formed Marriott Vacations Worldwide Corporation and later led legal support for MVW���s merger with ILG in 2018.

Marriott International Announces Significant Growth and Strategic Expansion in the Caribbean and Latin America in 2025
news_articles.content:
This is a paid press release. Contact the press release distributor directly with any inquiries.
Marriott International Announces Significant Growth and Strategic Expansion in the Caribbean and Latin America in 2025
PR Newswire
Wed, March 4, 2026 at 8:00 AM CST
Marriott International celebrated a momentous 2025 in its CALA region, with a record 94 signed deals and the addition of nearly 40 properties, strengthening its diversified portfolio from midscale to luxury, and all-inclusive to residential.
,
/PRNewswire/ -- Marriott International, Inc. (Nasdaq: MAR, "Marriott") announced an outstanding year of growth in its Caribbean and Latin America (CALA) region in 2025. The company achieved a record 94 signed deals in the region, adding 10,461 rooms to the pipeline, an increase of 40 percent in signed transactions and more than 30 percent in signed rooms compared to 2024. This performance reinforces Marriott's long-term commitment to diversified expansion and meeting evolving owner interests in the CALA region.
W Punta Cana Adult All-Inclusive
In 2025, Marriott expanded its portfolio with 39 properties and 4,292 rooms, growing its regional footprint to 555 open properties and more than 95,000 rooms across 37 countries and territories by year end.
"In 2025, Marriott solidified its leadership position in Caribbean and Latin America once again as the largest hotel company in the region, marked by another year of market-leading growth. This expansion reflects Marriott's adaptability
